msgs.text:
Turn 36 — the grade-two coefficient “curvature” is a Nielsen-coordinate artifact I used only the three files attached to this turn. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from F=P⋊G and the relators rS,rH. The attached problem statement gives P as the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and asks whether there is a continuous isomorphism ΓS→ΓH commuting with the canonical maps to G.

The new point is:the explicit grade-two term 2t+t2 in s2+1 is not an invariant obstruction source.It can be removed by an exact elementary Nielsen shear. So the next coefficient problem should not be built around the raw row(s2+1, s−1)=(2+2t+t2, t).

It should first be put into the exact normal form (2,t).This is a structural simplification of the coefficient-grade-two problem, not yet the full finite-coefficient proof.Recall the current local coefficient setup. On the dangerous Ef-piece,

R:=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,m=(2,t).

Turn 35 closed the whole first coefficient layerm/m2=(2,t)/(4,2t,t2):the first t-direction was contractible, and the first 2-direction had no surviving secondary obstruction in any root degree. The remaining gap began atm2/m3=⟨4, 2t, t2⟩,where Turn 35 proposed building a coupled grade-two ghost complex including the second-order term 2t+t2ins2+1=2+2t+t2.That was the right place to look, but the raw row is not the right coordinate system.

1. Exact row normalization

In R=Z2 [[t]], with t=s−1, we have (s+1)(s−1)=s2−1.

Hence(s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2.

Therefore the elementary matrixN=(1−(s+1)01)∈GL2(R)satisfies(s2+1, s−1)N=(s2+1−(s+1)(s−1), s−1)=(2,t).The determinant of N is 1, so this is an allowed G-operator Nielsen shear on the target-side free pro-2 operator group. This does not change the over-G isomorphism problem; it only changes the coefficient coordinates in which the correction complex is written. This is consistent with the earlier use of invertible H1 -matrices over Λ=Z2 [[G]], where the history already used GL2 (Λ)-changes to identify the abelianized relation rows.

So the grade-two term2t+t2=t(s+1)is a basis artifact. It is not a new curvature term that must be killed by a separate obstruction calculation.

2. What the grade-two complex becomes after normalization

After this shear, the local coefficient row is exactly (2,t).

On the associated graded ringgrm

R≃F2 [α,β],α=in(2),β=in(t),the leading coefficient row is exactly the regular Koszul row (α,β).Thus the grade-two coefficient layer should be organized by the regular-sequence/Koszul structure of (α,β), together with the actual Witt/Bockstein ghosts of group corrections.

A first-grade correction with coefficient λ∈⟨α,β⟩and primitive initial form W has the associated grade-two ghost shapeKλ2=1+αλW+λ2W[2]+⋯.

So the two basic coefficient generators behave as λ=α:(lower ghost,square ghost)=(α2 W, α2W[2]),and λ=β:(lower ghost,square ghost)=(αβW, β2W[2]).

This is the right replacement for treating 4,2t,t2 as three unrelated cases. The mixed term 2t, i.e. αβ, is paired with the t2 -square ghost of a t-correction. It is not an independent “middle” obstruction.Cross terms from sums of primitives contribute ordinary Lie brackets; as in the residual transgression arguments, those brackets die modulo the restricted-square quotient. The history used exactly this principle in the residual tower: in characteristic 2, the cross-bracket in(W+W′)[2]=W[2]+W′[2]+[W,W′]is ordinary Lie material and vanishes in the square quotient.

3. Consequence for the grade-two search

The grade-two coefficient problem is now narrower:

Do not test the raw 2t+t2row term.That term disappears under the explicit shear N. What remains is the normalized Witt–Koszul grade-two problem for the exact regular row (2,t).This gives three immediate simplifications.First, any obstruction functional that detects only the raw 2t+t2 contribution of s2+1 is not invariant. It is killed before the nonlinear Magnus/Bockstein calculation begins.

Second, the mixed 2t-piece must be tested together with the t2 -square piece as the ghost of a t-correction. A computation that separates 2t from t2 is the grade-two analogue of the earlier fixed-gauge mistakes.Third, the pure 4-piece is the second 2-Witt ghost, while the pure t2 -piece is the second t-ghost. The first coefficient layer already showed that the 2-direction has no surviving secondary obstruction in any root degree and that the first t-direction is contractible.The grade-two problem must now ask whether these two first-layer mechanisms are stable under the coupled Witt ghost λ↦(αλW,λ2W[2]),λ∈⟨α,β⟩.4. Status after Turn 36 This turn does not finish finite coefficient nonemptiness over every Artinian quotient of R≃Z2 [[t]]. The global compactness framework still requires nonemptiness of every finite coefficient-and-degree solution set, and the history correctly records that this is the remaining proof obligation after the residual tower was closed.But Turn 36 removes the most misleading part of the grade-two task. The apparent second-order row contribution2t+t2 is not a genuine obstruction term. It is eliminated by the explicit GL2 (R)-shear(1−(s+1)01).So the next computation should be made in the normalized coordinates with row (2,t),and should track the coupled Witt–Koszul ghosts, not the raw 4,2t,t2 expansion of the unnormalized row.

ESTABLISHED: On the dangerous Ef-coefficient piece R≃Z2 [[t]], the row (s2+1,s−1) is exactly GL2 (R)-equivalent to (2,t). Hence the grade-two term 2t+t2 in s2+1 is a Nielsen-coordinate artifact, not an invariant coefficient obstruction. The coefficient-grade-two problem must be rebuilt as the normalized Witt–Koszul ghost problem for the regular row (2,t).

GAP: The full finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma is still unproved. In normalized coordinates, one must still prove that the coupled grade-two Witt ghosts λ↦(αλW,λ2W[2]) for λ∈⟨α,β⟩, together with the already-cleared first-layer 2- and t-mechanisms, kill all actual m2/m3 defects.

NEXT: Build the normalized grade-two ghost complex for the exact row (2,t). Test the two genuine second ghosts λ=α and λ=β together, not as separate 4,2t,t2 cases; then try to promote the same normalized Koszul–Witt pattern to an induction over all mr/mr+1.[TURN36-GRADE2-ROW-CURVATURE-REMOVED]
